item.title: Provisional Licensing Information
item.content:encoded:
Note: Image source of "robot.png" is still unknown.

  • wormhole.png

Source: https://www.flickr.com/photos/nlireland/9148091321
License: No known copyright restrictions

Title: Sampson Island, Loop Head, Co. Clare
Author: National Library of Ireland on The Commons
Uploaded on: 2013 (Taken circa 1905)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there a reason why there are two different conflicting dates for the Addendum?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Positives
Positivies
  • This is incredibly creative, and superbly written with a lot of philosophical traction.
  • There is great cognitive dissonance on the part of the Foundation between idealism and realism; what they know the world probably should be like, and what it has to be like. A very strong "do as I say, not as I do".
  • The characters of SCP-1 and -2 are as humorous as they are disturbing, and their dark undertones are masterfully unveiled gradually throughout the interactions.
Question
Question

…the Xandromians must not use the Spire to view any other part of Earth,

  • How is the Foundation supposed to control what the anomalous spire shows the Xandromians? So far, the Xandromians have been able to witness a large swath of history, and how we will restrict their viewing "outside of the area directly around SCP-2670" is handwaved.

Good article.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Got two potential crosslinks here for your judgement/enjoyment. The first, is that in the final not there is a mention of Site-81 which can be made into an inline link to the facility dossier. Additionally, it was pointed up thread that the "Spire" mentioned in the article has a huge number of similarities between it and SCP-962, and I think that an inline link from the first mention of the Spire to that article would be a great way to world build while still remaining rather non-invasive. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Authorized by: Dir. Aktus, Site 81, O5-3, O5-4

To this:

Authorized by: Dir. Aktus, Site 81, O5-3, O5-4

Changing this:

Through conversations with SCP-2670-1, it has been determined that both SCP-2670-1 and SCP-2670-2 originate from an extraterrestrial world (Xandromius) containing a structure referred to only as the "Spire", which allowed the inhabitants of that world to view other worlds over great distances in real time.

To this:

Through conversations with SCP-2670-1, it has been determined that both SCP-2670-1 and SCP-2670-2 originate from an extraterrestrial world (Xandromius) containing a structure referred to only as the "Spire", which allowed the inhabitants of that world to view other worlds over great distances in real time.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Has anybody found the same mistake? The "Spire" gives Xandromians the ablity to "view other worlds over great distances in real time" , so "Codename: Paramount Regal" is not going to work form the begining.
For example, before the next meeting, Xandromians have already seen how religions spread on Earth nowadays. Then you tell them "all religions have been dissolved".

And there is a self-upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Failure to do so may result (and has previously resulted) in the torture and massacre of likely billions of sapient entities residing within SCP-2670.

Because Foundation totally hates torturing sapient enitites.

Jokes aside, it's nice to finally see working Ethics Commitee.

And "warmonger, slug-like entities uplifted by alien technology" is just screaming "Yeerks" to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God forbid that they witness anomalous activities. That would make a great tale, though. 2670 witnessing something like an SK dominance shift by bigfoot or 752 or 2000 resetting society yet again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Awesome. I feel like I just watched a Farscape marathon when reading this SCP.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …it's not an entity, it's a wormhole.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The scip itself is interesting, but to be honest, I hate the picture at the top. It *screams* Photoshop and started me off on a pretty negative footing for the article. It doesn't look like an amorphous entity. It looks like a Photoshop/GIMP/etc. brush that someone blobbed onto an old photograph (which is what it is). A simple blurry, smoke-like thing would have been better than that. I might even be able to stand the pattern if there was some sort of shading to make it look like it might possibly be part of the picture instead of a 2-dimensional painting, if SCP-2670-1 felt like wearing some weird plaid that day. I just can't upvote with that staring me in the face.

(Am I being a bit harsh? Maybe. But this is one of the very few SCP pictures that I've looked at and immediately hated outright.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed this, good job!

One thing I don't get, though, is why these aliens see humans as gods. I understand why they may consider humans as gods when they were first evolving but, 3000 years later? Doesn't make much sense. If they can create nukes and giant killing factories then they should be smart enough to realize that there are some serious harms from human society. Still, this scip gets a +1 from me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, this wouldv'e been an awesome 2000 entry. If I understand correctly, the theme was scifi, and this would've fit it perfectly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is the spire...
SCP-962?

Or at least related to it somehow? It's a spire that loves humanity and wants to remake a world in humanity's image, but does so in a horrifying way. It's a hell of a wild coincidence on your part if the connection isn't deliberate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoy the classic sci-fi elements in this, in the form of humanity viewed through an outside perspective and the issues that face it being the product of very deeply-held social constructs that simply aren't challenged in the course of most traditional fiction. Science fiction is at its best when it occupies this territory, IMO, and this article is a nice exercise in social critique.

Now, the conclusions reached in the article, in and of themselves, are not necessarily new or revolutionary. But what's interesting to me is that the Foundation chooses to enact the vision of society that it does. I think some of the precepts of the "ideal" society that the Foundation chooses to present could be fine-tuned and taken further to really start capturing the complexity of the issue and turn something interesting into something that is truly wickedly biting, but I like the direction enough to support the piece.

Interestingly enough, this also neatly sidesteps the "alien what talk and think like a human except kind of a little different" issue that I bring up every time someone writes about aliens. So there's that.

Also, the pictures are wonderfully weird, and help add to the mood of the piece.

Overall, solid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The why is unexplained, for a reason, but I can give some insight.

The Xandromians are not a peaceful people. They might think they are, but that's only because they evolved dramatically just over 3000 years ago. Before that, they were little more than a side dish for the cuisines of other species on Xandromius. Their reference is limited.

They see us as their gods, and see the actions we've taken against those we wish to oppress or have oppressed us, and they violently parody it because, again, despite being the dominant species on Xandromius, they're still wildly underdeveloped. They have no culture, just the worst parts of ours.

The protocol is so vague and extreme because the Foundation can't be certain how they'll react to it. Considering that they've already shown that they aren't great with examples, the best hope is that, with the information given as part of the protocol, they might tone it down even a little. Small amounts of genocide is better than large amounts of genocide.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wanted to upvote this, because I really liked the characters and setting of Xandromius, but there's a couple issues that bugged me enough neutral-vote.

View my nitpicks
– hide block

The fact that they focus so exclusively on negative aspects of our history seems kind of contrived. It's never really explained why they chose to emulate some things and ignore others.

Also, some parts of Paramount Regal sound more like they're pushing an agenda than addressing the problems in Xandromius. For example:

2.) The nations of Earth have denounced the destruction of natural resources for gain.

This is really broadly worded. Pretty much everything humans do involves destroying some natural resource for some sort of gain. Even breathing destroys a resource (oxygen) for a gain (survival). For all we know, the Xandromians might start suffocating themselves (or each other) now.

5.) All religions have been dissolved, and a global culture of humanistic virtue is being fostered.

This is also easily misinterpretable - knowing them, they'll probably start brutally oppressing all religions.

In general, the problem isn't really what they're emulating, it's how they're emulating it - always taking the worst, most violent aspects and exaggerating them. I'm not sure how these protocols address that. (To be fair, I'm not sure how they could address it, so maybe this is just their best attempt?)

So… as cool as the setting of Xandromius is, I have qualms with upvoting when the question of "why do they act this way" is pretty much unexplored.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fascinating.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Were the Vonnegut references intentional?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Failure to do so may result (and has previously resulted) in the torture and massacre of likely billions of sapient entities residing within SCP-2670. The details of Protocol Paramount Regal are detailed within Addendum 2670-D.

That bit bugs me as it feels like it is trying to evoke a emotional reaction. The rest is fine imo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nothing wrong with this but it just didn't grab me until I got to the List of Things We Have to Convince the Aliens are True. That made me grin, and I found myself rather liking the whole idea after all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That was a great read, and I was right there with Dr. Godwell during that 1946 transcript.

Is the "cylindrical monolith" supposed to be anything in particular that I'm not connecting to, or is it just a freak thing that came from somewhere that ended up in that universe?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh yeah. Derp.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: technically it's the first since it was posted on the 10th and yours on the 9th

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. Is today just, "Post your interdimensional portal skip day?" Seriously. This is the third one, and I'm the author of the 1st.
  2. Holy fuck. Like, holy fuck. Talk about misinterpretation.
  3. Wouldn't a story about paradise for the Xandromians make for a good Dystopia Contest entry?1
Footnotes
1. I already have a team and an idea, so go fucking crazy with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for the disturbing imagery. That's going to keep me up tonight.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow. I'm completely awed by this. Really nice job.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I swear I've seen the name "Xandromius" before. Is this the second such skip?

Anyway, I love this. It keeps getting better the more it goes. An extraterrestrial species awed by us for once. Frickin' brilliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Already said in chat, this is superb.

Fascinating, intruiguing, compelling, thoughtful. Humanity shown it's own failures in a grander scale. The repulsive reaction from the researcher in the second interview is amazing: this is a person who works for the SCP foundations, probably with a good level of clearance and the accompanying "I've seen some shit", and yet he can't contain himself.

item.title: Some deity that I used to know
item.content:encoded:
Wow I haven't written anything in a while. Hope this makes up for it.

I've thrown this one around for a bit, and finally got it to a point where I'm happy to post it. It was a lot of fun to write (and those images were fun to make!), and I hope you guys enjoy reading it as much as I did writing it.

Thanks to TwistedGears for checking this out in chat, the poor soul.

As always, your comments, critiques, and criticisms are always welcome!

First image is from here: https://www.flickr.com/photos/nlireland/9148091321/

The man in the second is here: https://www.archives.gov/nhprc/newsletter/2015/sept and is in the public domain.

robot image is from here: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Gogbot,_Enschede,_Robot_on_a_break.jpg
